text:
Once upon a time in a faraway land called Everdell, there lived a humble tailor named Larkspur. He crafted the finest cloth in the kingdom, from the softest mooncream cotton to shimmering silks. People from every corner would travel great distances to see his work, but what they always longed for was his special pattern: a flowing dress woven with stars.

Larkspur's workshop was a little cottage at the edge of the Whispering Woods, and it had no windows. He liked it that way; the world outside was filled with enough chaos. Inside, a loom lay stretched out before him, a tapestry of threads awaiting its life.

It was on a moonlit night when Larkspur heard a soft sigh that came not from his work but from within the woods themselves. A whisper drifted across the brook, and the trees seemed to lean a little closer. The dress material that had not yet taken form began to shimmer, forming
